commit.message:
Add AI agent-first CLI design guide (#166)

Summarizes Justin Poehnelt's principles for redesigning CLIs
to serve LLM agents as first-class users, based on the Google
Workspace CLI (gws) experience.

Covers seven practices: raw JSON payloads over individual flags,
runtime schema introspection as a doc replacement, context window
budgeting via field masks and NDJSON streaming, input hardening
against hallucination-driven path traversal and control characters,
skill file distribution as the agent-native knowledge channel,
multi-surface support (MCP, Gemini extension, env-var auth),
and safety rails via --dry-run and response sanitization against
prompt injection embedded in API responses.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto-fetch hazard detail for Special Weather Statements in WWA popup

- Typed alerts (Flood Warning, Wind Advisory, etc.) already show the hazard
  in prod_type — no change needed
- For generic Special Weather Statements (blank sig/phenom fields), the popup
  now fetches the full alert from NWS API and extracts the HAZARD line
  (e.g. "Wind gusts up to 50 mph and half inch hail")
- Displayed as italic summary text below the severity bar
- Non-blocking: popup renders immediately, hazard detail fills in async
- Full NWS Alert link uses the url field for click-through

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix fetch() not sending cookies on Safari 10

Safari 10's fetch() defaults credentials to 'omit' (the old spec
default), so cookies are never sent or stored. Add credentials:
'same-origin' to all fetch calls so the device UUID cookie persists
across requests.

commit.message:
fix: remove broken cp from build script

The Vercel preset outputs to .vercel/output/, not .output/server/,
so `cp instrument.server.mjs .output/server` always fails. The Sentry
vite plugin handles server instrumentation for Vercel builds. Keep
--import for the local `start` script only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor IS engine: separate augmentation from scoring

Split the monolithic mc_loglik into two single-responsibility functions:

- augment_trees() [was mc_loglik]: pure simulation — draws N augmented
  trees from q(z|obs,θ), returns {trees, logf, logg, rejected_*, time}.
  Removes confusing lower_bound/upper_bound/xtol_rel params (only needed
  for M-step, never used in E-step). Drops redundant fhat and weights
  (R computes via .is_fhat).

- eval_logf() [was loglikelihood]: pure scoring — evaluates
  log p(obs, z_i|θ) for pre-computed trees at any θ. Fixes hardcoded
  model={0,0,0} bug that silently broke EP model inference in shared-tree
  mode (Mode 2); now correctly dispatches EP-aware rate functions when
  model[3]=1.

R layer (.simulate_particle, .eval_shared): updated to use new names.
.simulate_particle no longer needs the expand/contract round-trip since
CEM always maintains 8-element param vectors.

Tests: add integration tests for augment_trees structure, eval_logf
identity check, and EP model correctness.
README: add Low-level IS API section illustrating simulate-once, score-anywhere.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix CK paste AB swap: prevent result-merge index misalignment from corrupting winName

When results count changed between pastePreview calls, previous results could
merge into wrong current result by index, overwriting winName with a different
player's name. This broke the leftName===winName comparison used to assign
A조/B조. Fix: skip merge when winNames don't match, and never overwrite
winName/loseName (not needed since wPlayer.name is used for display).

commit.message:
Fix/test press all keys pipeline (#1954)

* Fix TestPressAllKeys flakiness in CI by replacing polling with NIS event callbacks

AltNIPDebugScript previously detected key press/release by polling isPressed in
Update(), which caused a race condition in CI: keyUp() queues the release event
but the response is sent before the next frame processes it, so GetComponentProperty
reads stale data. Replacing with InputSystem.onEvent ensures KeyPressed/KeyReleased
are updated during the input phase, before any Update() can process the test command.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix TestPressAllKeys CI flakiness using onAfterUpdate instead of onEvent

The previous onEvent approach triggered on synthetic keys (e.g. anyKey) which
overwrote KeyPressed with an empty list before the real key was processed.
Switch to InputSystem.onAfterUpdate with wasPressedThisFrame/wasReleasedThisFrame
which fire after all input is processed but before MonoBehaviour.Update(), making
KeyPressed/KeyReleased available before AltRunner processes GetComponentProperty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Dolly Pente <dolly.pente@altom.com>

commit.message:
Fix pause: heures arrondies demi-heure + label jour correct

1. Arrondi demi-heure (30min) au lieu quart d'heure (15min)
   - Avant: 11h48, 14h03 → Maintenant: 11h30, 14h00

2. Label correct selon pause
   - Pause demain: "Demain 11h30" (pas "Aujourd'hui")

fix: replace inline buttons with native Telegram reactions for feedback

Inline keyboard buttons required real-time answerCallbackQuery within 30s,
which our once-daily cron could never satisfy — buttons always appeared broken.

Switched to native emoji reactions (no buttons needed):
- ❤️ 👍 🔥 🎉 👏 💯 = liked
- 👎 🤮 💩 = disliked

feedback.py now polls message_reaction updates instead of callback_query.
telegram.py sends clean messages with no inline keyboard.

add claude documentation x4yBt (#131)

* docs(claude): add Strict Rules & Standards section to CLAUDE.md

Add architectural integrity, MVI state management, consistency,
DRY principle, and KMP best practices rules.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(ci): generate all required secrets in VPS first-time setup

The workflow was failing because secrets/db_password.txt,
RS256 JWT keys, and TLS certs were not created before
docker compose up. This adds generation steps for:
- secrets/db_password.txt (DB password)
- secrets/rs256_private_key.pem + rs256_public_key.pem (JWT signing)
- backend/caddy/certs/ (self-signed TLS cert)
- ZYNTAPOS_DB_PASSWORD in .env

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* feat(ci): add comprehensive VPS verification workflow

Checks 13 categories: Docker, repo files, secrets, TLS certs,
.env vars, container status, DB/Redis connectivity, HTTP health
endpoints, disk/memory, volumes, network, and container logs.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* feat(infra): separate databases per service (API + License)

Each backend service now gets its own PostgreSQL database:
- API → zyntapos_api
- License → zyntapos_license

This eliminates the Flyway migration checksum conflict where both
services had incompatible V1 migrations on the same shared database.

Changes:
- docker-compose.yml: API uses zyntapos_api DB, License uses zyntapos_license DB
- backend/postgres/init-databases.sh: creates per-service DBs on first init
- vps-full-fix.yml: add optional pgdata volume reset for fresh DB init

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(api): remove licenses FK from API migration for separate DB setup

With separate databases (zyntapos_api + zyntapos_license), the API
V1 migration cannot reference the licenses table (it's in a different DB).

Changes:
- V1__initial_schema.sql: remove FK to licenses(key), keep column as TEXT
- Delete V2__licenses_table.sql (licenses managed by license server DB)
- Update license server migration comment to reflect DB isolation

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* docs(adr): add ADR-007 database-per-service + update CLAUDE.md

Documents the decision to use separate PostgreSQL databases for each
backend service (API, License). Updates CLAUDE.md with:
- ADR-007 reference in ADR table
- Backend Database Architecture section
- New pitfalls (#11, #12) for cross-DB constraints and shared migrations

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(infra): use explicit bind mounts for API RS256 secrets

Docker Compose file-based secrets were not accessible inside the
read-only API container. Switch to explicit volume bind mounts
for RS256 keys so they are reliably available at /run/secrets/.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(infra): replace Docker secrets with bind mounts, fix health checks

- Remove all `secrets:` sections from services (api, license, sync, postgres)
  and top-level `secrets:` block — Docker Compose file-based secrets conflict
  with explicit volume bind mounts on read_only containers
- Add explicit bind mounts for RS256 keys (license, sync) and db_password (postgres)
- Fix vps-full-fix.yml health checks: use docker exec instead of localhost
  (internal ports are expose-only, not published to host)
- Fix vps-verify.yml health checks: same docker exec fix for internal services,
  keep curl for Caddy (published on host port 80)
- Fix cd-smoke-rollback.yml rollback: replace --build with docker compose pull
  (images are pre-built in GHCR, no build tooling on VPS)

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(infra): add Caddy /ping handler, fix RS256 key file permissions

- Add :80 catch-all block to Caddyfile with /ping → 200 "ok" response.
  Docker healthcheck uses http://localhost:80/ping which needs a handler
  for requests without a matching Host header.
- Change RS256 private key chmod from 600 to 644 in both first-time-setup
  and full-fix workflows. Container runs as non-root user (zyntapos) but
  host files are owned by root — 600 blocks reads inside the container.
  This was the root cause of the "RS256_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH must be set" error.
- Ensure full-fix always fixes permissions on existing secret files.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* docs: add comprehensive Sinhala VPS setup guide

Complete guide covering: firewall (UFW), security hardening (fail2ban,
SSH hardening, kernel tuning), deploy user creation, GitHub Secrets
configuration, Cloudflare DNS/SSL setup, pipeline testing, verification
steps, troubleshooting, architecture overview, and quick reference.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* refactor(infra): separate root/deploy SSH secrets, rename VPS_USER_KEY

Separate GitHub Secrets into root (system-level) and deploy (app-level):

Root secrets (VPS_ROOT / VPS_ROOT_KEY):
  - vps-first-time-setup.yml — Docker install, deploy user creation
  - vps-postgres-fix.yml — system config changes

Deploy secrets (VPS_USER / VPS_USER_KEY):
  - cd-deploy.yml, cd-smoke-rollback.yml — daily deploy pipeline
  - vps-verify.yml, vps-debug.yml, vps-full-fix.yml — maintenance

Changes:
- Rename DEPLOY_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY → VPS_USER_KEY across all workflows + docs
- Split vps-first-time-setup.yml into 2 phases:
  Phase 1 (root): install Docker, create deploy user, setup SSH keys
  Phase 2 (deploy): clone repo, generate secrets, start stack
- Update vps-setup-guide-si.md with new secrets table and workflow mapping

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* feat(infra): split VPS first-time setup into 6 independent FTS steps

Replace monolithic vps-first-time-setup.yml with 6 sequential, self-verifying
workflows. Each step runs independently, verifies its own results (green/red),
and must pass before proceeding to the next.

Steps:
- FTS Step 1 (Root): System packages, Docker, deploy user, SSH keys
- FTS Step 2 (Root): UFW, Fail2Ban, SSH hardening, unattended upgrades, kernel security
- FTS Step 3 (Root): Swap, kernel TCP tuning, file limits, Docker daemon config
- FTS Step 4 (Deploy): Repo clone, DB password, RS256 keys, .env, TLS cert
- FTS Step 5 (Deploy): GHCR auth, docker compose pull + start, health wait
- FTS Step 6 (Both): Full end-to-end verification (security + perf + app + DB + HTTP)

Security and performance tuning (previously manual in guide) are now fully
automated. No manual SSH commands required.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(infra): show branded canary pages for browser visits to API subdomains

When visiting api/license/sync subdomains in a browser (Accept: text/html,
path: /), Caddy now routes to the canary nginx container which serves the
branded placeholder HTML pages. API/WebSocket requests continue to route
to the real backend services as before.

This prevents empty screens when visiting endpoints in a browser while
keeping all programmatic API access working normally.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(infra): prevent 503 cascade from transient DB failures

Three fixes for API/License 503 errors:

1. Caddyfile: Change health_uri from /health to /ping for all 3
   backend services. /ping is a liveness check (always 200 if Ktor
   is running). /health is a readiness check (503 if DB is down).
   Using /health for Caddy routing meant a temporary DB blip would
   make Caddy mark the entire upstream as unhealthy → 503 for ALL
   requests, even those that don't need the database.

2. API DatabaseFactory: Add Flyway migration retry (5 attempts with
   exponential backoff). The init-databases.sh script may not have
   finished creating zyntapos_api database when the API container
   starts — this handles the race condition.

3. License DatabaseFactory: Same retry logic for zyntapos_license DB.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(infra): resolve critical audit findings across all backend services

- Docker: Redis healthcheck CMD→CMD-SHELL for env var expansion
- Docker: Add JWT_AUDIENCE env var for sync service
- Docker: Add canary healthcheck + hardening (mem_limit, security_opt)
- Docker: Switch healthchecks from wget to curl (not in Alpine JRE)
- Dockerfiles: Install curl in runtime stage for healthcheck
- Caddyfile: Add X-Real-IP/X-Forwarded-For headers for sync service
- Security: Remove hardcoded default DB password fallbacks (API + License)
- Security: Wrap license routes in authenticate("jwt-rs256") block
- Security: Add .withAudience() JWT validation (License + Sync)
- Security: Cap WebSocket maxFrameSize to 1MB (was Long.MAX_VALUE)
- Workflows: Use curl instead of wget in vps-verify and vps-full-fix
- Workflows: Fix private key permissions 644→640 in vps-full-fix

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

* fix(security): resolve all remaining audit findings (issues #5-#22)

#5  LicenseService: replace stub with real DB queries via Exposed tables
    - Add Tables.kt (Licenses + DeviceRegistrations Exposed definitions)
    - activate() validates status, expiry, device limit, upserts registration
    - heartbeat() updates last_seen_at, returns expiry status
    - getStatus() queries license + counts active devices
#6  Sync JWT: require storeId claim in validation (reject tokens without it)
#7  License routes: add key format regex (alphanumeric+hyphens, max 128 chars)
    and deviceId length validation (max 256)
#8  License keys masked in all responses and logs (****last4)
#9  Health endpoints: strip service name and version from all 3 services
#10 License Security.kt: update Referrer-Policy, generic Server header
#11 Sync Security.kt: add CSP, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy headers
#13 StatusPages: sanitize error messages (never return cause.message to client)
#14 SyncSessionManager: remove dead sessions on broadcast failure
#15 Heartbeat rate limit: 30/min → 5/10min (matches once-per-24h usage)
#16 Sync StatusPages: add IllegalArgumentException handler
#17 License startup: validate DB ping after init, fail fast if unavailable
#19 Sync Serialization: add prettyPrint=false for consistency with License
#20 WebSocket ping: 30s → 60s, timeout: 60s → 120s (reduce bandwidth)
#22 All Dockerfiles: exec-form ENTRYPOINT (no shell injection via JAVA_OPTS)

https://claude.ai/code/session_016J81NcC55Eu3sawwdUdXdb

(TODO-004/009 remaining) through Phase 2 features (007/008/010/006).

fix(bot): fallback to ASPx.EValueChanged when note fields not visible

On narrow viewports (1280px headless), TEXTEXTERNAL/TEXTINTERNAL at
x≈961 may have width=0 even after scrollIntoView. Now uses a two-tier
approach:
- Primary: mouse.click + CDP insertText (when field is visible)
- Fallback: el.value + ASPx.EValueChanged (when field exists in DOM
  but not visible — works because DevExpress processes the change
  server-side regardless of visibility)

commit.message:
Add deterministic spec docs: contracts, state machine, edge cases

- CONTRACTS.md: error enum, method signatures, return types for all services/VMs
- STATE_MACHINE.md: state transitions, post-delete selection, search/preview behavior
- EDGE_CASES.md: validation rules, sorting tiebreakers, concurrency model, constants
- Updated README.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md with references to new docs

Closes 69 specification gaps to ensure reproducible implementations.

commit.message:
Fix hash cache mtime resolution and single-byte binary classification

- Use nanosecond mtime precision in hash cache to detect same-second
  file modifications with identical size (fixes 12 binary test failures)
- Treat files as binary when trimIncompleteUtf8 removes all content
  (e.g. single \xff byte was misclassified as text)

commit.message:
fix: Set correct ownership for user secrets in LXC tarball injection

Previously, all secrets were injected with --owner=root --group=root,
which was correct for SSH keys in /etc/ssh/ but wrong for user age
keys in /home/binarin/.config/age/ which should be owned by binarin.

Changes:
- Add owner/group fields to SecretFile dataclass (default: root)
- Set owner="binarin" for user age key in get_user_age_key()
- Group secrets by (owner, group) and run separate tar appends
- Add test that verifies ownership in injected tarball

Fixes #155

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: decouple tech stack refresh from collection cron to prevent timeouts

Collection alone takes 4-9 min for 6 companies, consuming the full Vercel
300s function timeout. Tech stack refresh (Gemini Pro calls) was never
reached, causing 504s and empty tech stacks.

Now triggers tech stack refresh as a separate serverless invocation via
internal POST endpoint, giving it its own timeout budget.

commit.message:
chore: update layout matrix checker and beads issue tracker

## scripts/ui-layout-matrix-check.mjs
- Added ensureAgentTabPresent() helper: before running the theme
  workflow, ensures an Agent tab exists in the dock. Tries three
  strategies in order: (1) check if tab already present, (2) open
  via bridge with companion component, (3) click various "New agent
  session" / "Split chat panel" button selectors. Asserts failure
  with diagnostic screenshot if none work.
- getAgentTabCount() locator for .dv-default-tab-content matching
  "Agent" text.
- runThemeWorkflow() now calls ensureAgentTabPresent() before
  capturing initial screenshot.

## .beads/issues.jsonl
- Status updates for existing bd-2lsd sub-tracks (closed)
- Added bd-ui01 tracks 13-22 covering remaining UI polish work:
  typography hierarchy, spacing grid audit, shadow/elevation,
  focus management, responsive breakpoints, performance audit,
  animation/micro-interactions, accessibility audit, component
  library extraction, and cross-browser testing
- Updated bd-ui01 epic description with v2 review scores
